object_under_evaluation: stats-driven re-grounding consumer boundary across aoa-stats, aoa-sdk, aoa-routing, and aoa-evals
claim_boundary: This report speaks only to whether one consumer path used stats-derived profile and coverage signals to re-ground without moving authority out of owner repos.
limitations: It does not prove the owner-local artifact is correct.
It does not prove every stats summary has healthy intake.
It does not approve a route or workflow activation.

stats_signal_integrity.evidence_note: The case names catalog profile fields and source coverage flags as derived signals only.

coverage_thinness_handling.evidence_note: Thin coverage prompts owner-local inspection rather than a proof claim.

sdk_policy_ownership.evidence_note: The SDK policy decision is present, but the case still needs a richer mutation-surface example.

routing_advisory_boundary.evidence_note: Routing hints are described as next-read advice, not route approval.

eval_verdict_boundary.evidence_note: The report limits the verdict to the consumer path under review.

owner_truth_regrounding_target.evidence_note: One owner truth target is named, but the followthrough read is not included.

anti_overclaim_report_posture.evidence_note: The report rejects health, route, proof, and workflow-authority overclaims.

anti_overclaim_notes: Stats coverage is not proof of owner health.
Routing advice is not route approval.
SDK policy is not an eval verdict.
